summary: BIONetZero Acceleration Programme – Call 1, Stage 1 (Ideation & Travel Grant). Access to stages 2-4 only via stage 1.
content: BIONetZero Acceleration Programme – Call 1, Stage 1 (Ideation & Travel Grant). Access to stages 2-4 only via stage 1.
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

Under this call, selected Small and Medium‑Sized Enterprises (SMEs) will participate in Stage 1 of the BIONetZero Acceleration Programme (Ideation & Travel Grant).

The objective of Stage 1 is to support SMEs in:

  1. refining and positioning their biosolution concept,
  2. strengthening their technical and commercial roadmap, and
  3. building relevant European and international networks.

Selected SMEs will be required to:

  1. participate in a mandatory BIONetZero kick‑off and networking event organised in Denmark,
  2. engage in structured networking, peer‑learning and knowledge‑exchange activities,
  3. work with a designated BIONetZero Key Account Manager (KAM) to shape a clear development pathway for their biosolution.

Stage 1 focuses on ideation, networking and preparation and does not include technology development activities.

Participation in Stage 1 is a mandatory prerequisite for applying for further financial support under the BIONetZero programme. However, acceptance into Stage 1 does not guarantee access to funding in subsequent stages.

The BIONetZero programme follows a four‑stage cascade funding model, with possible progression to:

  1. Stage 2: Tech Grant (TRL 4–6),
  2. Stage 3: Implementation Grant (TRL 7–8),
  3. Stage 4: Internationalisation Booster.

Progression between stages is subject to additional evaluation and selection.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

summary: Call for Pilots within the European data space for smart communities (submission round 4)
content: Call for Pilots within the European data space for smart communities (submission round 4)
citation: Data space for smart communities (deployment). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45358270
metadata.description:
['

The European data space for smart communities (DS4SSCC-DEP) action has previously launched three rounds of Calls for Pilots (CfP) to validate an EU-wide cross-sectorial data space in practice and to advance its development and implementation to support policy priorities of cities and communities within the European Union, including the green-digital transformation based on secure and sovereign data infrastructure. These calls launched 11 pilots to establish cross-border and cross-domain data space initiatives. This fourth and last call will seek to onboard approximately 10 additional pilots, validating even further the vision for a European data space for smart communities.

The pilots are set to build on and evolve the governance and technical blueprint established by the preparatory action for the European data space for smart communities, and evolved during the deployment action. This initiative aims to foster innovation towards a more resilient Europe, supporting local businesses and ecosystems, and emphasising ethical considerations and AI-enabled local solutions, thereby enhancing the conditions for citizens and communities across Europe.


Who Can Apply

The pilot call is specifically designed for local public administrations in the EU and Digital Europe Programme (DEP) Associated countries and partners working with them (e.g., companies, civil society organisations, academia). The consortia must be capable of executing a cross-sectorial and cross-border pilot activity, and co-finance at least 50% of the pilot activity.


Consortia Eligibility Criteria

  1. Eligible consortia must consist of at least two local or regional public administrations from different EU member states and/or DEP Associated countries, promoting cross-sectoral collaboration and dealing with the European Green Deal objectives.
  2. The local administrations can also involve other eligible parties working with them (e.g., companies, academia, civil society organisations and others).
  3. The pilot consortium members are subject to restrictions due to security reasons. Entities must not be controlled by an ineligible country. If there is a risk for such case, entities will be offered an opportunity to provide a guarantee.
  4. All entities (except entities that are validated as public bodies by the EU countries) have to fill in and submit an Ownership Control Declaration (OCD). OCD must be self-declared at proposal time, and if the pilot is awarded, the official OCD must be provided within 14 days from the notification date. If a chosen pilot consortium member is found, after assessment, to be controlled by a partner from a non-EU country, they will need to present a guarantee that will be assessed to allow or exclude participation in the pilot project. If a partner is found to be controlled by a DEP-associated country, they will also have to prepare a guarantee that will need to be assessed to allow or not allow their participation in the pilot.


Scope

Pilots launched through the Call for Pilots are expected to

  1. develop actual services created based on data sharing among data space participants,
  2. demonstrate the applicability of the DS4SSCC blueprint and 
  3. demonstrate feasibility of a federated European data space for smart communities. 

To support prospective applicants, interested local and regional administrations are encouraged to acquaint themselves with the currently active DS4SSCC-DEP local data spaces to explore the feasibility of replicating developed services and evolving these for local needs. As a result of pilot implementations, the DS4SSCC-DEP project will gather experiences from pilots and evolve the DS4SSCC blueprint.

The pilot consortia must create new data services from the data sharing across at least two areas specified below, but can also include other European Green Deal domains:

  1. predictive traffic management/sustainable mobility planning, establishing also synergies with the mobility data space and making use of the Sustainable Urban Mobility Indicators, where appropriate; 
  2. data-services related to weather, climate and extreme weather events to facilitate risk prevention, disaster resilience as well as climate change adaptation;  
  3. management of energy flows in a city/community specific context and in conjunction with other sectors; 
  4. zero pollution actions (e.g., air, water, soil pollution or waste);
  5. and any other domain falling under the New European Bauhaus initiative (urban design, building management, public services, nature-based solutions, amongst others.


Available funding and Co-Financing

Each pilot is planned to be funded with 250 000 - 500 000 euros by EU contribution to share amongst the consortium members of the pilot. The funding should be balanced proportionally amongst the pilot sites. 

The financial criteria for applicants involves a co-funding model. Applicants are expected to bear at least 50% of the project costs, as direct co-financing. This means that for any pilot proposed, the grant will cover only up to half of the total expenses. Applicants must directly indicate the co-financing as a dedicated cost in the budget for the pilot and demonstrate their capacity to provide it through their own resources. To be eligible, co-financing cannot include in-kind resources not explicitly monetized in the pilot budget, and all activities must observe relevant regulation, including state-aid rules and risks of double funding. As normally, all costs need to be actual, incurred during the pilot period time-frame and have to be auditable.

This approach ensures shared investment and commitment from both the funding body (EU) and the applicants, aligning with the principles of partnership and joint responsibility. The model is designed to encourage meaningful participation and investment in the project, ensuring that the applicants are equally invested in the success and sustainability of their initiatives. It\'s crucial for applicants to clearly articulate their funding strategy and show their ability to meet these financial requirements in their application.

The piloting consortium is expected to receive 50% of the total grant at the start, upon signing of the grant agreement, and the remaining 50% will be paid after approving the technical report and financial statement.

For more information about the Financial Framework, please refer to the Call for Pilots Manual at https://ds4sscc.eu/cfp-four  

']
metadata.title: ['Data space for smart communities (deployment)']

summary: JARVIS Open Call #2 - Co-development Track
content: JARVIS Open Call #2 - Co-development Track
citation: Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45634882
metadata.description:
['

The Co-development OC2 track aims to support the development of innovative technologies that extend the JARVIS ecosystem through new components and capabilities (TRL6), integrated and validated within the JARVIS pilots. Applicants are invited to submit proposals addressing one of the following topics, each linked to a specific JARVIS pilot:

Aeronautics Manufacturing (COLLINS Pilot)

  1. Topic #1: Large and deformable parts models supporting the implementation of impedance control-based co-manipulation
  2. Topic #2: Zero Interface Human-Robot Interaction in Agile Manufacturing
  3. Topic #3: Real-Time Robot Trajectory and Dress Pack Collision Avoidance

Automotive Manufacturing (TOFAS Pilot)

  1. Topic #1: Intelligent Mechatronic System for Collaborative Manipulation of Linear Flexible Components
  2. Topic #2: Wearable-Augmented Human Tracking for Uncertainty Reduction in Close-Range Human–Robot Collaboration
  3. Topic #3: Digital Safety Validation and Risk-Aware Design for Close-Range Human–Robot Collaboration

Nuclear Plants Decommissioning (EDF Pilot)

  1. Topic #1: Optimization of waste volume packaging
  2. Topic #2: Anomaly detection for teleoperation and skills execution

Offshore Inspection & Maintenance (EQUINOR Pilot)

  1. Topic #1: Robust self-localization and mapping with mobile robots in semi-outdoor process plant environments
  2. Topic #2: Online automatic detection of damages on metal/concrete infrastructure and/or operational features
  3. Topic #3: Autonomous intervention with mobile manipulator using Foundation Models

Selected projects are expected to:

  1. Design and develop a solution addressing one of the JARVIS topics
  2. Integrate their solution within the corresponding JARVIS pilot via ROS2-compatible interfaces
  3. Deploy and validate the solution in the relevant JARVIS testbed
  4. Demonstrate the added value of their technology for the JARVIS pilot, improving processes such as productivity, flexibility, safety, or human-robot collaboration

The work will be structured in three Sprints:

  1. Sprint 1 – Requirements: – Detailed definition of system architecture, integration approach, demonstration and validation scenarios, and key performance indicators
  2. Sprint 2 – Development & Deployment: Development of the solution and initial deployment in the pilot environment
  3. Sprint 3 – Integration & Validation: Full integration, testing, and validation in real conditions

Applicants are expected to deliver:

  1. A functional module prototype (TRL6)
  2. Validation results based on defined KPIs
  3. Documentation of integration with JARVIS pilots
  4. A clear pathway for further exploitation and scalability

All solutions must be compatible with the JARVIS ecosystem and contribute to extending its capabilities within industrial environments.

']
metadata.title: ['Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)']

summary: JARVIS Open Call #2 - External Pilots Track
content: JARVIS Open Call #2 - External Pilots Track
citation: Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45634882
metadata.description:
['

The External Pilots OC2 track aims to validate innovative AI-enabled solutions in real industrial environments (TRL7) and validate the JARVIS modules in new application domains. Applicants are invited to submit proposals addressing one of the following challenges:

  1. Worker-Centric Collaborative Robotics Applications for Physically Demanding Tasks in Manufacturing Environments
  2. Adaptive Human–Robot Interaction in Complex Manufacturing Processes
  3. Remote Human-Robot Interaction for Inspection and Maintenance of Plants, Infrastructure Areas and Linear Infrastructure
  4. Inspection and Maintenance of Industrial and Public Sector Assets and Equipment
  5. Human–Robot Interaction in Emerging and Strategic Domains

Selected projects are expected to:

  1. Design and implement a pilot solution addressing one of the five JARVIS challenges
  2. Integrate one JARVIS module into their system via ROS2-compatible interfaces
  3. Deploy and validate the solution in a real operational environment provided by the Use Case Provider
  4. Demonstrate the added value of JARVIS technologies in improving existing processes, such as productivity, flexibility, safety, or human-robot collaboration

The work will be structured in three Sprints:

  1. Sprint 1 – Design: ·Detailed definition of the system architecture, integration approach with the selected JARVIS module, demonstration scenarios, validation strategy, and performance indicators.
  2. Sprint 2 – Development & Deployment: Development of the solution and initial deployment in the pilot environment
  3. Sprint 3 – Integration & Validation: Full integration, testing, and validation in real conditions

Applicants are expected to deliver:

  1. A functional pilot demonstrating the proposed solution (TRL7)
  2. Validation results based on defined KPIs
  3. Documentation of integration with JARVIS components
  4. A clear pathway for further exploitation and scalability

All solutions must be compatible with the JARVIS objectives and contribute to demonstrating its applicability in new industrial contexts.

']
metadata.title: ['Industrial leadership in AI, Data and Robotics – advanced human robot interaction (AI Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)']

summary: NGI Fediversity open call (2026-06F)
content: NGI Fediversity open call (2026-06F)
citation: Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45635989
metadata.description:
['

Our software supply chains are a silent miracle, the result of millions of hours spent every year packaging, compiling and distributing software. From this vast firehose of bits come the apps and services we depend on every day. Subsequently, billions of hours are spent by users on dealing with everything from simple updates to complex system administration. This vast effort is necessary to keep oneself abreast of the never-ending supply of potential vulnerabilities that arrives with just a slight delay. Users that fail to responsively track new releases of every deployed service and each of its dependencies, are bound to land themselves or others in trouble sooner rather than later.

Deploying internet based services is much tougher than we can afford. No matter how user friendly or well-written software is, enabling the community at large to maintain internet services by themselves at scale has proven to be consistently challenging. Achieving high availability scenario\'s is even more of a dark art. No wonder, people just give in and surrender their data.

Fediversity is a comprehensive effort to bring easy-to-use, hosted cloud services with service portability and personal freedom at their core to everyone. It wants to provide everyone with high-quality, secure IT systems for everyday use. Without tracking, without exploitation, in a way that runs everywhere and scales effortlessly. Fediversity is based on NixOS, a disruptive Linux distribution with a unique approach to package and configuration management. Built on top of the Nix package manager, NixOS is completely declarative, makes upgrading systems reliable, and has many other advantages. Because it is reproducible, it is ideally suited for complex deployment scenario\'s where consistent behaviour, stability and configurability matter.

One such "complex" deployment scenario is running state-of-the-art services for the Fediverse, like PeerTube, Mastodon, Owncast or Lemmy — especially if you want to for instance add services like live chat or transcoding. But even running more traditional services like modern e-mail servers with possible whistles and bells can be daunting. The same holds for deploying a VPN, private cloud storage, wiki, etc. Fediversity will enable all of these use cases, and more — finally bringing these to the market in a way that is as conveient as using a hosted service.

Fediversity will deliver an ambitious development effort, but this is a vast domain with many more challenges than what any preconceived effort could tackle by itself. This is why we invite your contribution to help us reshape the state of play, and together create an open, trustworthy and reliable internet for all.

This is your opportunity to make a real difference. You tell us how your project can help Fediversity go harder, better, faster, stronger. In order to enable you to make such contributions, we will award small to medium-size R&D grants towards solutions that bring the next generation of mobile solutions closer. We are seeking project proposals between 5.000 and 50.000 euro\'s — which should get you on your way. If the project works out well you can subsequently scale up in other programmes funded by NLnet such as NGI0 Commons Fund. Noteworthy fact: many projects which are to be deployed inside Fediversity were themselves bootstrapped on precisely such a grant from NGI, and now it is your turn.

Note that if your project is not related to the Nix functional package management system, we suggest you submit to another open call such as NGI Zero Commons Fund.

']
metadata.title: ['Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA)']

summary: NGI Zero Commons Fund (2026-06Z)
content: NGI Zero Commons Fund (2026-06Z)
citation: Next Generation Internet Fund (RIA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45635952
metadata.description:
['

NGI0 Commons Fund, co-funded from the European Union\'s Horizon Europe research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 101135429, foresees as an eligible activity the provision of financial support to third parties, as a means to achieve the objectives of the Next Generation Internet initiative and to contribute to a resilient, trustworthy and sustainably open internet.


It is clear that the commons model works, and continues to gain ground. Free and open source software and hardware, open standards, open data & AI, open science, creative commons and open educational resources are democratising innovation and learning, and are together driving society and industry forward at an unprecedented pace. But it is also obvious that there are major gaps that need to be fixed still. The answer to market failure and dystopian runaway technology lies in collective action and public investment.


This is where NGI Zero Commons Fund come into play. The goal of this new fund is to help deliver, mature and scale new internet commons across the whole technology spectrum, from libre silicon to middleware, from P2P infrastructure to convenient end user applications. We have a holistic, full-stack approach, simply because there is no other way. If we want to reclaim the public nature of the internet and yield the full benefits from technology as a society, we need to have full coverage — period.


Only though enough critical mass of shared building blocks and collective resources will our governments, businesses and civil society be able to secure their security and long term operational availability. The same approach that empowers individuals to take full control of their digital lives, will also steer towards more fair, innovative economies and societies. So let\'s make it happen!


The changes we want to see are vast, and there is much technical debt to resolve as well. We are looking for troubleshooters that help society tackle the hard but very very important challenges ahead. Ultimately we believe in the power of the quadruple helix — the collaboration of the public sector, aligned companies, academia and civil society — to restore the internet (and our economies) back to health. Together we can create a strong and open internet for all, and bring about new paradigms like the European Commissions vision for Web 4.0 which aim to harness upcoming techologies like virtual and augmented reality, generative AI and intelligent mediators in a responsible way so they may sustainably serve society.


We need new ideas and disruptive technologies, while at the same time evolving and growing existing technologies that are still future-proof.


Have a look at other NGI Zero programmes to see the kind of efforts we mean, but don\'t be afraid to send something completely different if you think you can contribute to the topic — it really is an open call.


Project results always become available under a free or open source license, so anyone can read and validate the source code, and anyone can use the code or open data to create solutions that fits their own purposes. The right to reuse and right to repair not only allow for unrestricted scrutiny and permission-free innovation, but also help to reduce e-waste. And the use of standards enables interoperability and redundancy in implementation to reduce the risk of compromise and failure.


NGI0 Commons Fund is made possible with financial support from the European Commission\'s Next Generation Internet programme, under the aegis of DG Communications Networks, Content and Technology.

Additional funding is made available by the Swiss State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation (SERI).

']
metadata.title: ['Next Generation Internet Fund (RIA)']

summary: Net Zero Technology - Demo Projects Open Call #1
content: Net Zero Technology - Demo Projects Open Call #1
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

This Open Call supports SMEs, either as manufacturing end-users or solution providers, in implementing advanced processes and technologies that accelerate the transition towards net-zero manufacturing.

Proposals must address a concrete manufacturing use-case, demonstrating direct application within an industrial production environment.

Actions shall focus on the deployment and demonstration of mature, near-to-market solutions in real industrial environments (TRL 7–8). The objective is to bridge the gap between applied research and market uptake by enabling scalable, operational implementation.

Projects must :

  1. Deliver reductions in greenhouse gas emissions and environmental impact;
  2. Improve energy efficiency and/or optimize resource use;
  3. Integrate innovative and qualified digital technologies (e.g., IoT, AI etc.);
  4. Be clearly embedded in a manufacturing context and demonstrate measurable results.

Eligible activities. The following activities may be supported under this Open Call, provided they are directly linked to the objectives of the project and contribute to the demonstration and deployment of Net-Zero Technologies in a manufacturing context:

·Demonstration and pilot activities

  1. Deployment and testing of technologies in real or operational industrial environments
  2. Pilot lines, protoptypes, demonstrators, or proof-of-concept validation at TRL 6–8
  3. Integration of technologies into existing production systems

·Technology implementation and adaptation

  1. Customisation and integration of NZT solutions into manufacturing processes
  2. Retrofitting or upgrading industrial equipment to improve energy efficiency or reduce emissions
  3. Process optimisation and digitalisation (e.g. IoT, AI, data-driven monitoring)

·Digital solutions for sustainability

  1. Adopting or deployment of digital tools for energy monitoring, optimisation and management
  2. Consumption/resource reduction, tracking and valorisation systems
  3. Digital twins or predictive maintenance solutions improving resource efficiency

·Circular economy implementation

  1. Reuse, recycling or recovery of materials within production processes
  2. Development of circular production systems or industrial symbiosis approaches
  3. Reduction of raw material consumption and waste generation

·Testing, validation and performance assessment

  1. Measurement and verification of environmental impact (e.g. GHG emissions reduction)
  2. Life-cycle or performance assessments directly linked to the demonstration
  3. Validation of scalability and replicability of the solution
  4. Activities that fill the gap between demonstration and commercialization.

Applicants are most welcome to also include upskilling, and/or internationalization and value chain strengthening activities, and/or promotion activities, to show the scalability of the solution and contribute to Europe’s leadership in sustainable manufacturing.

Projects must clearly indicate :

  1. TRL before project start
  2. TRL expected at project completion

Projects should typically aim to progress from TRL 5–6 toward TRL 7-8.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

summary: LLM-BRIDGE – Venture Incubation Programme - Open Call for Start-ups – 1st Call
content: LLM-BRIDGE – Venture Incubation Programme - Open Call for Start-ups – 1st Call
citation: Support to the implementation of Multi-Country Projects (MCPs). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47038289
metadata.description:
['

Task description

The Venture Incubation Programme (VIP) call aims to select 30 early-stage GenAI startups to participate in a 12-week incubation programme taking place in summer 2026. The programme will support the development and scaling of innovative ventures working with large language model (LLM) technologies.

Selected startups will receive structured support throughout the programme, including entrepreneurial mentoring, technical guidance, access to high-performance computing (HPC) resources and data infrastructures, as well as regulatory and responsible AI orientation. The VIP also seeks to accelerate the commercialisation of LLM-based solutions, promote responsible AI innovation in line with key European frameworks (such as the AI Act, Data Act, GDPR, and the Digital Decade Targets), and foster cross-border collaboration and inclusivity within the European AI ecosystem.

Following the completion of the programme, up to five of the best-performing startups will be awarded a €25,000 Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) prize to further advance their development and growth.

The programme will be implemented by a selected incubation partner in collaboration with the LLM-BRIDGE consortium members.

']
metadata.title: ['Support to the implementation of Multi-Country Projects (MCPs)']

summary: NGI TALER open call (2026-06T)
content: NGI TALER open call (2026-06T)
citation: Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45635989
metadata.description:
['

In the digital economy, payments play a critical role. Yet online payment systems tend to allow for far less privacy than paying with a bank note or coins, especially when using proprietary solutions like Google Pay or Apple Pay. When interacting with the offline economy comes into play, the alternative of paying with all kind of volatile cryptocurrencies isn\'t a viable option either.


NGI TALER is a project funded by the European Commission and the Swiss State to roll out a new electronic payment system that benefits everyone: people, merchants, banks, financial authorities, auditors and anti-corruption researchers. The project doesn\'t have to start from scratch either, but builds on the strong foundations of GNU Taler — the privacy-preserving digital payment system developed by the GNU community and Taler Systems SA. This offers privacy for those that make payments, while enforcing transparency on those that sell. By providing micro payments at very low overhead, GNU Taler permits internet business models to shift away from advertising revenue or subscription models, especially for online publishers. No-risk transactions can lower transaction fees and open online payments for the underbanked population and citizens marginalized from digitalisation.


Part of the budget of NGI TALER (15% to be precise) is reserved for open calls to fund additional free and open source efforts that are aligned with the topics and approach of NGI TALER. We invite your contributions to help reshape the state of play of digital payment systems, and to help create an open, trustworthy and reliable internet for all. Of course you can contribute exciting new capabilities to GNU Taler itself, build auxiliary tools or work on user experience, but you could also be developing integrations into FOSS applications and open standards (enabling P2P micropayments in for instance an instant messenger, open social media platform or video conferencing tool), or work on improvements to infrastructure components like merchant backends. Together with the rest of the Next Generation Internet initiative, we move towards restoring and subsequently maintaining European sovereignty and to secure democratic ownership of our digital societies.


In order to enable you to make such contributions, we will award small to medium-size R&D grants towards technology commons that empower users, and establish a new generation of privacy-friendly digital payments systems. The programme will run between now and November 2026, but budgets are limited — so grab your opportunity.


Project results must be made available under a free and open source license, so anyone can read and validate the source code, and anyone can use the code to create technology that fits their own purposes. We are seeking project proposals between 5.000 and 50.000 euro\'s — if the project works out well you can subsequently scale up in other programmes funded by NLnet such as NGI0 Commons Fund. Reliable, low-cost, secure and resource efficient payments should become the \'new normal\' of the internet, something ordinary users should not have to worry about — users should be in control. So let\'s make it happen. Note that GNU Taler itself was funded through such Next Generation Internet projects, so there is every opportunity to grow.


Through the open calls, we assist independent researchers and developers to join in and create powerful new technologies and put these in the hands of future generations as building blocks for a fair and democratic society. Help us build a sustainable and open economy that benefits all, and submit your idea today. Not sure whether your idea fits? Don\'t be afraid to send something completely out-of-the-box if you think you can contribute to the topic (and it fits with the eligibility criteria): it really is an open call.


Want to learn more about Taler before you submit a proposal, or have other questions? Take a look at the GNU Taler technology, the main NGI Taler site - or join the GNU Taler Integration Community Hub to have a chat about your ideas.

']
metadata.title: ['Pilots for the Next Generation Internet (IA)']

summary: Open Call for innovative projects enhancing the valorisation of agri-food biomass
content: Open Call for innovative projects enhancing the valorisation of agri-food biomass
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

This call targets innovative SMEs developing solutions that increase the valorisation of biomass, contributing to more sustainable and resilient agri-food ecosystems through the development of new processes, services and products.

This Innovation Call includes financial support for projects especially focusing on the development, adoption, and commercialisation of innovative upcycled ingredients for food and non-food applications, products, business processes, and technologies (including digital) that maximise the valorisation of agri-food biomass and side-streams. These projects should have a minimum Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 6.

1.The Innovation Open Call targets:

  1. SMEs in the agri-food production and/or processing sector;
  2. SMEs in other sectors (ex. cosmetics, nutraceuticals, etc) that valorise biomass/by-products/side-streams from the agri-food sector;
  3. SMEs in other sectors (ex. cosmetics, nutraceuticals, etc) that valorise their own biomass/by-products/side-streams for the agri-food sector;
  4. SMEs offering solutions and/or technology for the valorisation of biomass/by-products/side-streams from the agri-food sector (development and application of new technologies or processes).

2.The applicant’s project must lead to the implementation of either a new process, a new product, or a new service or a combination of them.

3.Proposals can be submitted either by a single SME or by an SMEs consortium with up to three (3) SMEs.

4.Applicants can be SMEs registered at EU Member States and countries associated to the Single Market Programme.

5.Projects must have a minimum Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of at least 6.

6.For Single SME projects, the maximum lump-sum grant per project (1 SME) is €60.000.

7.For collaborative projects, the maximum lump-sum grant per project (2-3 SMEs) is €90.000.

8.Innovation grants must be matched by at least 15% private financial contribution (debt or own financial resources), with proof required.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

summary: PERIFORMANCE OPEN CALL FOR BOTTOM-UP STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT INITIATIVES IN CANCER RESEARCH & CARE
content: PERIFORMANCE OPEN CALL FOR BOTTOM-UP STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT INITIATIVES IN CANCER RESEARCH & CARE
citation: Experimental local action for EU missions: knowledge institutions as focal points of transdisciplinary research and innovation activities with European outreach. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46945037
metadata.description:
['

The PERIFORMANCE Open Call supports bottom-up stakeholder engagement initiatives that strengthen participation, trust, and collaboration in cancer research and care across Europe. The call invites organizations to design and implement innovative local or regional activities that actively involve patients, researchers, healthcare professionals, communities, policymakers, and other stakeholders in dialogue and co-creation processes related to cancer research, care, and survivor support.

Funded initiatives may include participatory and co-creative formats, educational and capacity-building activities, artistic or cultural engagement, and communication or outreach actions that foster awareness, dialogue, and inclusion. Activities should address local needs while aligning with the objectives of the EU Mission on Cancer, such as improving understanding of cancer, prevention and early detection, diagnosis and treatment, and quality of life for patients and families.

The call aims to support innovative engagement practices that explore emerging technologies in cancer research, including the ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence and the European Health Data Space. By funding up to 15 initiatives, PERIFORMANCE seeks to generate scalable best practices, strengthen inclusive participation, and promote responsible innovation, ultimately contributing to more equitable, transparent, and socially responsive cancer research and healthcare systems across Europe.


']
metadata.title: ['Experimental local action for EU missions: knowledge institutions as focal points of transdisciplinary research and innovation activities with European outreach']

summary: Phortify Grants for the Be Part of Phortify program directed to women - Pilot Call
content: Phortify Grants for the Be Part of Phortify program directed to women - Pilot Call
citation: Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46813062
metadata.description:
['

About ICFO

ICFO is a CERCA research centre member of the Barcelona Institute of Science and Technology, founded in 2002 by the Government of Catalonia and the Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya · Barcelona Tech, both of which are members of ICFO’s board of trustees along with philanthropic entities that have played a critical role in the advancement of the institute. Located in the Mediterranean Technology Park in the metropolitan area of Barcelona, the institute currently hosts 450 people, organized in 25 research teams that use 80 state-of-the-art research laboratories. Research lines encompass diverse areas in which photonics plays a decisive role, with an emphasis on basic and applied themes relevant to medicine and biology, advanced imaging techniques, information technologies, a range of environmental sensors, tunable and ultra-fast lasers, quantum science and technologies, photovoltaics and the properties and applications of nano and quantum materials such as graphene, among others. In addition to four consecutive accreditations of the Severo Ochoa national program for top research excellence, ICFOnians have been awarded 16 elite ICREA Professorships as well as 53 European Research Council grants. ICFO is highly proactive in fostering entrepreneurial activities, spin-off creation, and creating collaborations and links between industry and ICFO researchers. To date, ICFO has helped create 13 start-up companies.  

About Phortify 

Phortify - Photonics Education Network for Next-Gen Innovation and Digital Skills Excellence for Industry and Society - is dedicated to the Enhancement and Harmonisation of Photonics education and training across Europe. Phortify offers a two-year European Master’s in Photonics Engineering, combining advanced coursework, hands-on laboratory training, and cutting-edge digital skills development, structured mobility windows and industry placements. Students who complete the Phortify track receive an official Phortify Certificate in addition to their Master’s degree. Students can enrol in a Phortify-related master programme at any of the 7 participating universities. In addition, Phortify provides stand-alone modules with harmonised standards for quality assurance and certification, offering high flexibility and mobility for both students and industry professionals. Finally, Phortify organises mentoring programmes for women postdocs and early PIs as well as one-day immersive experience events for prospective students, providing a unique opportunity to explore photonics and its applications. 

About the Phortify Funding Framework 

Phortify’s Funding Framework aims to widen participation in photonics by supporting students and staff who are traditionally underrepresented in STEM. This includes women and gender minorities, students from low-income or socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds, first-generation university students, and learners with disabilities or specific support needs. The programme also encourages participation from regions with limited access to advanced higher education, as well as those belonging to cultural or minority groups that may be underrepresented in higher education. By addressing these dimensions of diversity, Phortify helps ensure a more inclusive and equitable community of future photonics professionals. Participants selected for Phortify funding receive financial support for studies and mobility pathways within the participating master programmes, or for mentoring programmes and STEM experience workshops organised by one of the Phortify partners. 

']
metadata.title: ['Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas']

summary: GreenMantis Innovation Support Scheme
content: GreenMantis Innovation Support Scheme
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

The goal of GreenMantis is to financially support manufacturing and energy SMEs to develop innovative projects around three Net-Zero Technologies:

  1. Electricity Grid Technologies
  2. Energy System-Related Energy Efficiency Technologies
  3. Transformative Industrial Technologies leading to Decarbonisation

Funded projects will have to make SMEs more sustainable by :

  1. Developing New to Firm products, services or processes
  2. Leading to the development of business process innovation tied to technological adoption
  3. Improving their overall energy efficiency

These projects will have to be from a TRL 6 to 8 and with a duration of 9 months. This Open Call will support cross-national and cross-sectoral projects between companies and mainly SMEs. Proposals will therefore have to be built around a consortium.

Eligible Consortium :

  1. At least 2 SMEs located in two different EU Member States or Associated Countries to the SMP-COSME programme, from 2 different sectors (NACE code C, D or J, M with a proven activities related to manufacturing)
  2. Larger companies or universities can be part of your consortium but will not receive funding.

Bear in mind that only SMEs will receive funding from GreenMantis.

Under this call, at least 10 projects with a maximum funding of 100,000€ per project will be awarded, with an individual cap of 60,000€ per SME.

GreenMantis will allow within its funded projects activities such as :

  1. Technology development and prototyping (TRL 6–8)
  2. Pilot testing and validation in operational environments
  3. Demonstration activities
  4. Integration of Net-Zero technologies into manufacturing processes
  5. Business process innovation directly linked to technological adoption
']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

metadata.description:
['

Eligible Countries and geographic areas:

  1. Italy along the Via Francigena, stretch Pavia–Rome and 10 km buffer zone;
  2. Greece along the mountainous paths of Olympos, Kissavos, Mavrovouni;
  3. Bulgaria along the Sultan’s Trail in Thrace Tourism Region and Thrace Tourism Region;
  4. Türkiye along the Lycian Way, from Fethiye to Antalya, Türkiye and 3 km buffer zone.

Partnership Composition

  1. 2 to 5 independent legal entities
  2. At least 50 % must be tourism SMEs1. At least one tourism SME should be located along (or in buffer zone of) eligible walking routes.
  3. Each entity can participate in only one funded partnership.
  4. Partnerships may be of the following types:
  5. Only tourism SMEs
  6. Tourism SMEs and other tourism entities (DMOs, associations, sole-traders etc.)
  7. Tourism SMEs and Cross-sectoral partners (culture, agri-food, digital, IT, sole-traders, etc.)

Projects must contribute to:

  1. Sustainability and green transition
  2. Digital transformation
  3. Business resilience and risk management
  4. Inclusive and accessible trail tourism

Permissible Activities (Examples)

  1. Development of new tourism products or services
  2. Digital tools, booking systems, mapping platforms
  3. Relevant software licences (operational efficiency, customer experience)
  4. Sustainable mobility solutions
  5. Green technologies and certifications
  6. Market studies and business plans
  7. Staff training and upskilling
  8. Communication and marketing campaigns
  9. Risk management planning

⚠ Equipment costs capped at 30% of total budget

']
metadata.title: ['Transitioning to a more sustainable and resilient ecosystem – empowering tourism SMEs']

metadata.description:

Selected projects will be launched in November 2026 having an implementation timeframe of 18 months (until April 2028). The implementation period will be divided in 3 phases:

  1. Phase 1 - Design [Deliverable: Activity plan] (November 2026 – February 2027) –Payment of 20% of the total grant.
  2. Phase 2 - Development [Deliverable: Report on implemented activities in accordance with the OC requirements] (March 2027 – January 2028) – Payment of 50% of the total grant.
  3. Phase 3 - Impact and validation [Deliverable: Report on evaluation of impact and replicability of the tested processes and technologies (February 2028 – April 2028) – Payment of the final 30% of the total grant.

In all selected projects, payments are deliverable based, meaning that the Sub-grantees have to deliver a specific report after the end of each phase in order to receive the payment (no pre-financing is foreseen).

']

summary: AUTOASSESS Open Call #2 for Tech Innovators
content: AUTOASSESS Open Call #2 for Tech Innovators
citation: Increased robotics capabilities demonstrated in key sectors (AI, Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44131435
metadata.description:
['

AUTOASSESS aims to find solutions to ensure the structural safety of ships, oil and gas and other marine infrastructures. Currently, this task is done by human surveyors who must climb into confined areas such as ballast tanks or cargo holds, which represent extremely dangerous GNSS-denied environments. The inspection is a physically demanding task, done in tight enclosed spaces, difficult to access, with low/no light, slippery surfaces and with low/no oxygen and toxic gases.

The overall goal of AUTOASSESS is to remove human surveyors and workers from dangerous and dirty confined areas of offshore structures by employing an autonomous robotic system that exceeds human capabilities and is able to perform maritime vessel classifications. To address this goal, AUTOASSESS aims to employ a robot and remove human surveyors out of harm’s way, while at the same time obtaining an accurate, repeatable, and quick vessel inspection.

The overall objective of the AUTOASSESS FSTP mechanism is to further promote the development & deployment of the AUTOASSESS solution through the engagement of innovative technological third parties, providing solutions to specific project’s challenges or proposing new ideas/ concepts that generate value to AUTOASSESS.

The AUTOASSESS Open Call #2 for Tech Innovators will fund nine (9) projects addressing key challenges aligned with the overall AUTOASSESS project. The awarded projects will start their activities in July 2026 and have an implementation period of nine months, divided into three stages:

  1. Stage 1 – Plan [1 month, 40% of the grant]
  2. Stage 2 – Develop and Integrate [6 months, 40% of the grant]
  3. Stage 3 – Assess [2 months, 20% of the grant]

At the end of each stage, sub-grantees must provide a report that will lead to the indicated payment associated to the stage. All sub-grantees will be provided with monitoring and other technical support.

']
metadata.title: ['Increased robotics capabilities demonstrated in key sectors (AI, Data and Robotics Partnership) (IA)']

summary: GATE5.0 Open Call for Agrifood & Technology SMEs – Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP)
content: GATE5.0 Open Call for Agrifood & Technology SMEs – Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP)
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

GATE5.0 (Green and Digital Transitions for a Sustainable European Agrifood Ecosystem) aims to accelerate the green and digital transformation of the European agrifood sector.

Through this Open Call, the project provides Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) to consortia composed of one Agrifood SME and one Technology SME to jointly develop and validate innovative solutions addressing key agrifood challenges.

Selected projects will participate in the GATE5.0 Acceleration Programme, structured as an innovation funnel (SEED, SPROUT and HARVEST phases). Projects must start at a minimum TRL 6 and aim to achieve advanced validation and market readiness.

The programme supports the development, piloting and scaling of green and digital solutions contributing to sustainability, resource efficiency and digitalisation of the agrifood ecosystem.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

summary: RENEW-BOOSTER EUROCLUSTER CALL N°1 FOR FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO THIRD PARTIES
content: RENEW-BOOSTER EUROCLUSTER CALL N°1 FOR FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO THIRD PARTIES
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

The two calls will be structured through two distinct funding strands.

Strand A (Challenge-driven) follows a two-step process linked to predefined industrial challenges. In Step 1, up to three SMEs per challenge receive €10,000 (with minimum 20% private co-funding) to develop and validate a proof of concept (TRL 6–7). After evaluation and a final pitch, one SME per challenge advances to Step 2 and receives €50,000 (with minimum 40% co-funding) to further develop and scale the solution (TRL 8–9).

Strand B (Open Call) follows a single-stage selection process. SMEs apply bottom-up and can request up to €10,000, €30,000, or €60,000 depending on project maturity, with increasing private co-funding requirements. Projects run up to six months.


Find more here: call text.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

summary: CYSSDE Open Call 3: Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Assessment
content: CYSSDE Open Call 3: Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Assessment
citation: Preparedness support and mutual assistance. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46040389
metadata.description:
['

The CYSSDE 18-Month Support Programme

Upon selection and signature of the Sub-Grant Agreement, beneficiaries will receive up to €200,000 in funding, disbursed across an 18-month tailored Support Programme. Each beneficiary will be paired with a dedicated CYSSDE expert mentor and guided through four operational stages:

• Stage 1: Execution Plan (Month 1)

Beneficiaries will define a detailed roadmap based on their successful proposal, establishing clear goals, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), deliverables, and resource allocation.

• Stage 2: Developing Testing Scenarios (Months 2–6)

This phase focuses on the technical preparation for the external assessments. Activities include strategic planning, designing tailored testing scenarios, and setting up or installing the necessary systems, tools, and environments.

• Stage 3: External Assessments with End-Users (Months 7–15)

The core operational phase. Beneficiaries will deploy their services to the market, executing a minimum of 10 penetration tests and vulnerability assessments for their selected end-users (critical infrastructures, essential services, or SMEs).

• Stage 4: Outputs and Sustainability Services (Months 16–18)

The final stage focuses on maximising impact and mitigation. Beneficiaries will be guided in generating publications (anonymised where necessary), managing responsible vulnerability disclosures to operating organisations or manufacturers, and providing ongoing risk monitoring services.

']
metadata.title: ['Preparedness support and mutual assistance']

summary: CR. EU. IN. HERITAGE: Cultural Heritage Innovation Grants
content: CR. EU. IN. HERITAGE: Cultural Heritage Innovation Grants
citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

Applicants are invited to submit proposals for collaborative innovation projects specifically focused on the Cultural Heritage Value Chain (CHVC). Projects must contribute to European strategic autonomy and the twin transition (green and digital).

Proposals must align with one of the following strand:

  1. Strand 1: Developing new-to-the-firm products or services.
  2. Strand 2: Adopting new-to-the-firm business processes.

For further information, please check the call document.

Key Requirements:

  1. Consortium: The Step 2 proposal must be submitted by a consortium of at least two SMEs from eligible SMP countries.
  2. Technology: Projects must demonstrate the application of EU-based digital solutions/technologies and/or the engagement of Innovation Leaders.
  3. Duration: The project implementation period is fixed at 12 months. If selected under Step 1, the SMEs will have to complete a set of mandatory activities (between May-June 2026) to be considered eligible to apply under Step 2.
  4. Projects require a minimum 15% co-financing contribution from the applicants based on the total project budget (e.g., a grant of €77,000 or €120,000 plus the mandatory 15% contribution). This contribution is mandatory for all participating SMEs within a project. While 15% is the minimum requirement, providing a higher percentage of co-financing will result in additional points during the evaluation process.


Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Applicants can consult Frequently Asked Questions at the following link.

Applicants are advised to consult them regularly, as the document is continuously updated.

']
metadata.title: ['Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery']

citation: Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/43549596
metadata.description:
['

As part of its activities, FuseNet supports the FuseNet member institutions with the organisation of mini-workshops. These are small-scale workshops that aim to connect young talents with experienced researchers. They have the set-up of masterclasses on a dedicated topic, include interaction between experts and students and are organised for either MSc or PhD students. Such events typically last 2–5 days. This call is for mini-workshops that are to be organised before the end of this year.

']
metadata.title: ['Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027']

summary: FuseNet Support of Master Students going on an internship abroad
content: FuseNet Support of Master Students going on an internship abroad
citation: Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/43549596
metadata.description:
['

This support package is meant to facilitate MSc students in fusion who want to go abroad for an internship, thereby stimulating quality of education through exchanges. The internship can take place at a fusion research centre or university involved in fusion research. This place is not limited to Europe. Students registered at universities that are a FuseNet member and/or are doing their internship at hosts that are a FuseNet member, and their home university is located in one of the EUROfusion member states can apply for financial support for their internship here.

The financial support consists of a fixed amount of travel support (depending on the travel distance) and accommodation/living support (depending on the internship duration). The application has to be submitted at least 2 weeks before and within 6 months of the start of the internship. Other sources of funding for the internship (scholarships or grants, compensation or salary from the host institute, funds from other EU support schemes, etc.) need to be specified. Funding can also be restricted in cases where funding has been received from FuseNet in the past (see the terms and conditions on the FuseNet webpage).

']
metadata.title: ['Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027']

summary: FuseNet Support of Master Students in attending Educational Events
content: FuseNet Support of Master Students in attending Educational Events
citation: Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/43549596
metadata.description:
['

This support scheme is launched to support and facilitate Master students to take part in educational activities external to their own organization. It allows Master students to participate in fusion Master classes, summer schools, and other short-term events with a dominant educational character. All conferences are excluded from support except when the Master student has his/her own contribution at the event (see terms and conditions for details).

Students registered at universities that are a FuseNet member and/or are seeking to attend educational activities at hosts that are a FuseNet member, and both the home university and the educational event are located in one of the countries that are part of the EUROfusion member states, can apply here for financial support to take part in a fusion-related educational training activity.

The financial support consists of a fixed amount of travel support (depending on the travel distance) and subsistence support (depending on the duration). The (training) activity has to take place at a university group or research institute in one of the EUROfusion members states or at a FuseNet member institute. The application has to be submitted at least 2 weeks before the actual start of the educational event. The educational event has to start no later than 6 months after application.

']
metadata.title: ['Euratom-Adhoc-2021-2027']

summary: Call for CSOs initiatives “Code Success” Fund
content: Call for CSOs initiatives “Code Success” Fund
citation: Call for proposals for civil society organisations active on protecting and promoting Union values: call to intermediaries (giving financial support to third parties). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46357639
metadata.description:
['

The Code Success Fund will support initiatives that raise awareness of EU rights and values, promote democracy and active civic participation, protect media freedom and counter disinformation, and work for equality, inclusion, and non-discrimination.

The supported initiatives may include:

-Awareness raising, including communication and information campaigns,

-Activities aimed at countering disinformation regarding EU values and policies,

-Various activities fostering civic dialogues at local level on topics related to EU rights and values,

-Activities in the local community to create coalitions and partnerships between civil society organizations,

-Civic participation/advocacy with the aim of contributing to decision-making processes (at regional or local level),

-Training, exchange of experience and good practices, mentoring, knowledge transfer, and other activities aimed at increasing the capacity of civil society organizations and other stakeholders on topics related to EU values,

-Activities that promote interethnic and intercultural dialogue in the community

-Providing free advice and monitoring implementation of EU policies, rights, and values at the local level in accordance with the EU Treaties and the Charter of Fundamental Rights.

']
metadata.title: ['Call for proposals for civil society organisations active on protecting and promoting Union values: call to intermediaries (giving financial support to third parties)']

summary: Phortify Grants for Mentorship workshop on career growth and professional development - Pilot Call
content: Phortify Grants for Mentorship workshop on career growth and professional development - Pilot Call
citation: Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46813062
metadata.description:
['

About Phortify

Phortify - Photonics Education Network for Next-Gen Innovation and Digital Skills Excellence for Industry and Society - is dedicated to the Enhancement and Harmonisation of Photonics education and training across Europe. Phortify offers a two-year European Master’s in Photonics Engineering, combining advanced coursework, hands-on laboratory training, and cutting-edge digital skills development, structured mobility windows and industry placements. Students who complete the Phortify track receive an official Phortify Certificate in addition to their Master’s degree. Students can enrol in a Phortify-related master programme at any of the 7 participating universities. In addition, Phortify provides stand-alone modules with harmonised standards for quality assurance and certification, offering high flexibility and mobility for both students and industry professionals. Finally, Phortify organises mentoring programmes for women postdocs and early PIs as well as one-day immersive experience events for prospective students, providing a unique opportunity to explore photonics and its applications.

About the Phortify Funding Framework

Phortify’s Funding Framework aims to widen participation in photonics by supporting students and staff who are traditionally underrepresented in STEM. This includes women and gender minorities, students from low-income or socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds, first-generation university students, and learners with disabilities or specific support needs. The programme also encourages participation from regions with limited access to advanced higher education, as well as those belonging to cultural or minority groups that may be underrepresented in higher education. By addressing these dimensions of diversity, Phortify helps ensure a more inclusive and equitable community of future photonics professionals. Participants selected for Phortify funding receive financial support for studies and mobility pathways within the participating master programmes, or for mentoring programmes and STEM experience workshops organised by one of the Phortify partners.

']
metadata.title: ['Specialised Education Programmes in Key Capacity Areas']

citation: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/47108154
metadata.description:
['

Under the PowerBoost stream, the only eligible activity is the implementation of an innovation audit, that will be reimbursed with a total of EUR 2000 once the audit is approved, aimed at identifying challenges and opportunities for the Applicant organisation to contribute to grid flexibility, sustainability, and digitalisation in the energy sector.

The innovation audit should help the SME understand its current processes, technologies, and operational gaps, and define areas where innovation or new technology adoption could improve efficiency, flexibility, or integration with renewable energy systems.

The innovation audit must result in a Challenge Description Report, prepared using the official template provided by GreenGrid.

']

summary: BlueActionAA – Transition Agenda Call (BAAT-01)
content: BlueActionAA – Transition Agenda Call (BAAT-01)
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

BAAT-01 aims to distribute a total of EUR 1,000,000 in the form of lump sum grants (The grant is awarded to the consortium coordinator only.)

·Expected project budget: Up to EUR 100,000

·Expected project duration: 6 to 18 months.

·Expected: Minimum 10 projects will be selected.

1.Eligible beneficiaries

The applicant must propose activities that are demonstrably contributing to the Mission objectives.

1.1.Country

To be eligible for funding, the applicants (and co-applicants) must be legally established in one of the eligible countries, which include:

·The Member States (MS) of the European Union (EU)

·The Overseas Countries and Territories (OCT) linked to the Member States

·Horizon Europe associated countries at the time the BlueActionAA grant entered into force (01/09/2025)

While no restriction applies, lead applicants (project coordinators) are particularly encouraged from the following Atlantic/Arctic Basin countries and territories: France, Ireland, Spain, Portugal, Norway, Iceland, Greenland, Faroe Islands, UK and Canada.

1.2.Entity

Entities must correspond to the first BlueActionAA target groups:

·Regional and local public authorities;

·NGOs, foundations (public and private), professional associations, civil society and citizen associations. Such entities should be engaged in local development and nature conservation activities;

·SMEs and Large Enterprises;

·Cultural and Educational organisations;

·Relevant EU Research Infrastructure, research organisations and academia.

2. Consortium

·Applications from individual entities (single applicant submission) or from multi-partner consortia are eligible.

·Consortia of up to 6 partners may apply.

·BlueActionAA consortium partners, their affiliated entities, and their employees, are not eligible and are, therefore, not allowed to submit a proposal or receive any financial support through the open call.

3. Maximum funding

Organisations can receive grant aid only once from BlueActionAA.

The maximum amount of grant that can be awarded to any individual organisation participating in a Transition Agenda project is EUR 75,000 (hence, single applicant projects cannot request funding of more than EUR 75,000).

This rule applies to the consortium coordinator as well as to the co-applicants.

Language in which application should be submitted: English

Web address for further information: www.blueactionaa.eu  

Contact:

·info@blueactionaa.eu for any questions you might have related to topic specific questions, and;

·technical.support@blueactionaa.eu for technical questions concerning the submission platform SmartSimple (operated by ESF) and/or the cascade call grant process

Open call submission platform:  For a first connection, click here ; For subsequent connections: click here

']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: Call for applications n. 1/2026 for the assignment of 6 scholarships for the enrolment in the Joint Master’s Degree in International Cybersecurity and Cyberintelligence – A.Y. 2026/2027
content: Call for applications n. 1/2026 for the assignment of 6 scholarships for the enrolment in the Joint Master’s Degree in International Cybersecurity and Cyberintelligence – A.Y. 2026/2027
citation: Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45114064
metadata.description:
['

The selected third parties are future students of the Joint Master’s Degree in International Cybersecurity and Cyberintelligence (MICAC), a two-year interdisciplinary programme jointly delivered by 4 universities of the Cyberactioning consortium.

The Master’s programme provides advanced training in cybersecurity and cyberintelligence, combining technical, analytical and strategic competences. Students develop skills in areas such as cyber threat analysis, secure systems, data protection, cybercrime investigation, risk assessment, intelligence analysis and the ethical, legal and policy dimensions of cybersecurity.

Through a strong practice-oriented approach, including hands-on laboratories, applied projects and international mobility, students acquire digital and transversal skills that are highly relevant for the European digital labour market.

The financial support contributes to the payment of tuition fees and enables the participation of selected students in the educational and training activities foreseen in the programme, in line with the objectives of the Cyberactioning project to strengthen advanced digital skills and foster high-level expertise in cybersecurity and cyberintelligence in Europe.

https://arqus-alliance.eu/study-in-arqus/joint-masters-programmes/master-in-cybersecurity-cyberintelligence/cybersecurity-about/

']
metadata.title: ['Specialised education programmes or modules in key capacity areas']

summary: Excel4Pro Open Call
content: Excel4Pro Open Call
citation: Excellence Hubs. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45640341
metadata.description:
['

Selected projects will be launched in November 2026 having an implementation timeframe of 15 months (until December 2027). The implementation period will be divided in 3 phases:

  1. Phase 1 - Design [Deliverable: Activity plan] (November 2026 – February 2027) - Payment of 30% of the total fund
  2. Phase 2 - Development [Deliverable: Report on Results/Outcomes] (March 2027 – July 2027) - Payment of 50% of the total fund
  3. Phase 3 - Validation [Deliverable: Final report on community-associated activities] (August 2027 – January 2028) - Payment of the final 20% of the total fund

In all selected projects, payments are deliverable based, meaning that the subgrantees have to deliver a specific report after the end of each phase in order to receive the payment (no pre-financing is foreseen).

']
metadata.title: ['Excellence Hubs']

Data protection/GDPR 

Participants applying to ACHIEVE Master programmes (incl. FSTP applicants) are required to provide certain personal information to the application system (i.e contact information, ID, email and postal addresses). All applicants must read and agree with EIT Digital Privacy policy and Master School Terms and Conditions and consent to allow EIT Digital to store and process their personal information. The documentation to read explains the grounds for personal data processing, the purpose for personal data collection, and the measures in place to protect personal data.

For more details about the FSTP applicants’ data collection and retention, please refer to ACHIEVE’s Data Management plan.

Under GDPR, all FSTP applicants have the right to: Access, correct, or erase their data, or withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, applicants may redirect their request to: privacy@28digital.eu  


']

summary: Accelerating Food Sustainability - through Household Dietary Shifts, Trust and Transparency, and Innovations in Circular Food Processing Systems
content: Accelerating Food Sustainability - through Household Dietary Shifts, Trust and Transparency, and Innovations in Circular Food Processing Systems
citation: European partnership on sustainable food systems for people, planet and climate. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/45652931
metadata.description:
['

Central to the FutureFoodS Partnership is the ambition that tackling the complex challenges making current European food systems unsustainable requires mobilizing knowledge, actors and resources through a food systems approach. An approach of this kind is important as it propounds a holistic view when addressing wicked problems, acknowledging enduring dependencies between food system actors, as well as identifying drivers and leverage points within systems. The proposals submitted to this call should clearly delineate and characterise the particular food system under inquiry. What are its key actors? What are the main dependencies within the system? What levers may induce enhanced sustainability of the system? How does the chosen one connect to other food systems?

Applicants shall demonstrate the originality of their approach and build on the results of existing EU level initiatives. Funded projects shall foster synergies with other initiatives and shall have the capacities to take a changing policy environment into consideration - also during the life-time of the project.

Each application submitted under this call should focus on one of the three call topics listed below. The topic chosen should be clearly indicated in the proposal:

1 Domestic food practices for enhancing sustainable and healthy diets

2 Towards diverse, sustainable and circular food processing systems

3 Importance of trust and transparency

In addition, the type of projects, Exploratory Research Project or Accelerating Innovation Project, chosen should be clearly indicated in the proposal.


Please note that primary production (on land and sea/water), such as growing food, agricultural production and other specific aspects related to it, as well as diseases related to nutrition and lifestyle are not in focus in this partnership. These aspects are covered in other Horizon Europe partnerships. However, dependencies and impacts related to these aspects may need to be reflected upon in a project to fully embrace a food systems approach.


The Call Announcement highlights the aim, expected approach and topics of the call. It also details the countries/regions participating in funding the call, the eligibility criteria, submission, evaluation and selection procedures, and modalities for projects funding and reporting.

Specific funding regulations apply depending on the funding organisations: each partner applying to the call must ensure that its contribution to the overall project conforms to these regulations, as reported in the document “Annex I - National/regional regulations” available for download on the submission platform.


']
metadata.title: ['European partnership on sustainable food systems for people, planet and climate']
