summary: BlueActionBANOS Open Call for Community-Led Actions
content: BlueActionBANOS Open Call for Community-Led Actions
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

In this Open Call, BlueActionBANOS will select up to 15 Community-Led Actions led by area-based or interest-based stakeholder groups proposing impactful solutions that are innovative for their community, aimed at achieving at least one or several interlinked Mission Ocean and Waters objectives in the Baltic and North Sea (BANOS) basin. The activities should foster a participatory approach that empowers local stakeholders, builds on their local know-how and application of sustainable practices, and encourages their long-term commitment to Mission Ocean and Waters objectives.

What BlueActionBANOS offers:

Selected Community-Led Actions will receive:

  1. financial support between between EUR 200 000.00 and up to EUR 2 000 000.00 per Action, based on the budget included in the full proposal form; the maximum amount to be granted to each entity within a Community-Led Action is EUR 500 000.00;
  2. ongoing Action support provided by dedicated Project Key Accounts and tailored technical assistance, defined during onboarding workshops, for a maximum duration of 24 months.

The support programme will be divided into three stages, with clearly defined milestones and milestone review procedure:

  1. Stage 1 - Individual Integrated Project Plan (IIPP), duration: 2 months (M1-M2) deliverable - completed IIPP
  2. Stage 2 Mid-Term Review, duration: 12 months (M3 - M14), deliverable - CLA Mid-Term Report
  3. Stage 3 Final Review, duration: 10 months (M15 - M24), deliverable - CLA Final Report

The Grant will be paid as a lump sum. Payments depend on the successful and timely completion of each stage of the work planned and outlined in the Individual Integrated Project Plan (IIPP) developed in Stage 1 of the support programme.

']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: BlueActionBANOS 1st Open Call for Transition Agendas
content: BlueActionBANOS 1st Open Call for Transition Agendas
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

In this 1st Transition Agendas Open Call, BlueActionBANOS will select ~10 Transition Agendas to develop strategic roadmaps for sustainable systemic shifts, targeting Mission Ocean objectives in the Baltic and North Sea. Projects will develop multi-year plans for areas like decarbonising coastal zones, circular blue economy, sustainable blue tourism, ecosystem-based fisheries management, marine restoration, and climate adaptation, plastic and nutrient pollution reduction and other goals that support Mission Ocean objectives. Eligible applicants are individual entities or consortia of up to three, registered in one or more BANOS countries, including regional/local authorities, NGOs, educational establishments, local companies, and/or research organisations.

What BluActionBANOS offers:

Selected Transition Agendas will receive:

  1. financial support up to EUR 100 000.00 per Transition Agenda, based on the budget included in the full proposal form; the maximum amount to be granted to each entity within a Transition Agenda is EUR 60 000.00
  2. ongoing Action support provided by dedicated Project Key Accounts and tailored technical assistance, defined during onboarding workshops, for a maximum duration of 18 months.

The support programme will be divided into three stages, with clearly defined milestones and milestone review procedure:

  1. Stage 1 - Individual Integrated Project Plan (IIPP), duration: M1 - M2, deliverable - completed IIPP
  2. Stage 2 Mid-Term Review, duration: M3 - M12, deliverable - completed Transition Agenda Roadmap
  3. Stage 3 Final Review, duration: M13 - M18, deliverable - Transition Agenda Implementation Report.

The Grant will be paid as a lump sum. Payments depend on the successful and timely completion of each stage of the work planned and outlined in the Individual Integrated Project Plan (IIPP) developed in Stage 1 of the support programme.

']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: BlueActionAA – Community Led-Pilot Action Call (BAAC-01)
content: BlueActionAA – Community Led-Pilot Action Call (BAAC-01)
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

BAAC-01 aims to distribute a total of 7,000,000 Euros in the form of lump sum grants (The grant is awarded to the consortium coordinator only.)

  1. ·Expected project budget: 200,000 Euros to 2,000,000 Euros
  2. ·Expected project duration: 6 to 24 months.
  3. ·Expected: Minimum 5 projects will be selected.

The main focus of BAAC-01 is to ensure Long-Term Impact. The long-term sustainability and impact of the proposed projects will be the most important criterion in the evaluation procedure

Eligible beneficiaries

The applicant must propose activities that are demonstrably contributing to the Mission objectives.

Country

To be eligible for funding, the applicants (and co-applicants) must be legally established in one of the eligible countries:

  1. ·The Member States (MS) of the European Union (EU)
  2. ·The Overseas Countries and Territories (OCT) linked to the Member States
  3. ·Horizon Europe associated countries at the time the BlueActionAA grant entered into force (01/09/2025)

The consortium coordinator must be based in an area within an eligible country (see list above) in the Atlantic or Arctic basin, e.g. France, Ireland, Spain, Portugal, Norway, Faroe Islands, Greenland, Iceland.

Entity

Entities must correspond to the BAAAC-01 identified Target Groups:

  1. ·Regional and local public authorities;
  2. ·NGOs, foundations (public and private), professional associations, civil society and citizen associations. Such entities should be engaged in local development and nature conservation activities;
  3. ·SMEs and Large Enterprises;
  4. ·Cultural and Educational organisations;
  5. ·Relevant EU Research Infrastructure, research organisations and academia

Consortium

  1. ·To be eligible for funding, a consortium needs to have at a minimum 2 partners from different Target Groups represented. It is not possible to apply as a single entity.
  2. ·The maximum per consortia is 12 partners.
  3. ·BlueActionAA consortium partners, their affiliated entities, and their employees, are not eligible and are therefore not allowed to submit a proposal to receive any financial support through the open call

Maximum funding:

The maximum amount of funding that can be granted to a single organisation for all BlueActionAA calls combined cannot exceed €500 000

This rule applies to the consortium coordinator as well as to the co-applicants.

Language in which application should be submitted: English

Call documentation associated with the Community-Led Action Call is available here: Community-Led Action Call 

Web address for further information: www.blueactionaa.eu

Contact:

  1. info@blueactionaa.eu for any questions you might have related to topic specific questions, and;
  2. technical.support@blueactionaa.eu for technical questions concerning the submission platform SmartSimple (operated by ESF) and/or the cascade call grant process
']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: TASC-RestoreMed Open Call Type B: Transition Agenda Development (TAD)
content: TASC-RestoreMed Open Call Type B: Transition Agenda Development (TAD)
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

Transition Agenda Development (TAD): What’s in it for You?

Directly influence Policy and Governance for Mission Ocean

The Transition Agenda Development call offers you the opportunity to deliver a concrete contribution to EU, national, regional and local authorities in fulfilling environmental and regulatory objectives linked to the Mission “Restore our Ocean and Waters by 2030”.

Through your project, you will identify governance gaps, address regulatory barriers and help create the political and legal conditions necessary for the long-term sustainability and large-scale deployment of innovative solutions across the Mediterranean Sea basin.

Apply individually or in a focused strategic consortium

You may apply as:

  1. An individual legal entity, or
  2. A consortium of up to two (2) legal entities,

This flexible structure allows you to tailor expertise precisely to your policy and governance objectives.

Engage high-level policy, research and stakeholder actors

This call is particularly relevant if you are active in:

  1. Regional and local authorities focused on governance and policy development
  2. NGOs and associations engaged in policy advocacy and multi-stakeholder dialogue
  3. Research Performing Organisations conducting legal and regulatory harmonisation studies

Your project will be embedded in a real Mediterranean basin governance context, ensuring direct relevance and practical policy uptake.

Operate within a clear Mediterranean and EU framework

To participate, applicants must be legal entities with a valid VAT registration established in:

  1. European Union Member States, or
  2. Horizon Europe associated countries.

In addition:

  1. At least one applicant must be legally established in the Mediterranean Sea basin and
  2. All project activities must be implemented within the Mediterranean Sea basin, in marine, transitional and freshwater bodies.

This guarantees strong territorial relevance and alignment with regional policy needs.

Deliver high-impact strategic and policy outcomes

Your Transition Agenda project will enable you to:

  1. Develop strategic roadmaps for marine, coastal and inland water protection and restoration
  2. Support pollution reduction and elimination, and contribute to decarbonisation and circular economy targets
  3. Analyse regulatory and governance gaps, including emerging technology needs
  4. Engage stakeholders in structured policy and regulatory assessments
  5. Provide short-, medium-, and long-term policy recommendations to improve and adapt existing legal frameworks

These outputs will significantly strengthen your organisation’s policy influence, strategic visibility and institutional impact at local, national and EU levels.

Official Call Link

All relevant documents and the online application form are available at:

https://tascrestoremed.eu/transition-agendas-development/

']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: TASC-RestoreMed Open Call Type A: Community-Led Actions (CLA)
content: TASC-RestoreMed Open Call Type A: Community-Led Actions (CLA)
citation: Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46939903
metadata.description:
['

Community-Led Actions (CLA): What’s in it for you?

Deploy your innovation at operational scale

The Community-Led Actions call offers selected applicants the opportunity to scale up and deploy innovative solutions under real operational conditions in the Mediterranean Sea basin. Projects are expected to progress from pilot-level maturity (TRL 5) to operational demonstration (TRL 6–7 or higher), delivering verifiable environmental benefits for marine and freshwater ecosystems.

Access dedicated EU financial support

Successful applicants will receive EU-funded financial support covering the full scope of implementation activities, enabling the effective deployment, monitoring and demonstration of Community-Led Actions aligned with the Mission “Restore our Ocean and Waters by 2030”.

Implement with a structured consortium model

Applications must be submitted by a consortium of at least two legal entities, ensuring both technological excellence and real-world uptake:

  1. A Provider responsible for delivering the core technology, solution, or knowledge.
  2. An Adopter responsible for integrating and applying the solution in an operational environment.

This structure ensures direct deployment, validation and transferability of results. These required roles may be fulfilled by the two legal entities or secured through clearly defined subcontracting arrangements.

Engage with Key Mediterranean Stakeholders

Eligible applicants and beneficiaries include, inter alia:

Ports and marinas; municipalities and regional authorities; aquaculture and aquafeed producers; conservation and scientific organisations; circular economy companies and start-ups; and research and education institutions.

All funded actions must be implemented in the Mediterranean Sea basin, guaranteeing strong local relevance and community engagement.

Strengthen long-term sustainability and market readiness

Beyond technical implementation, the CLA programme supports applicants in:

  1. Developing a robust business and impact case,
  2. Enhancing investment readiness,
  3. Positioning solutions for replication and follow-up financing.

Deliver measurable contribution to EU policy objectives

Funded projects are expected to demonstrate clear, quantified contributions to Mission Ocean targets, including for example:

  1. Reduction of plastic litter and microplastics,
  2. Restoration of marine and freshwater ecosystems,
  3. Strengthened local capacity for sustainable blue economy solutions.

All projects must define Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and measurable outcomes.

Submission Process and Reference Documentation

Applications must be submitted exclusively through the F6S online platform, in full compliance with the official templates and procedural requirements.

Applicants should carefully consult all official call documents prior to submission. In particular, the Annex 1 Guide for Applicants constitutes the primary reference document, as it sets out:

  1. The complete evaluation methodology,
  2. The scoring system and thresholds, and
  3. The selection and ranking procedure applied by independent external experts.

Familiarisation with this document is essential for the preparation of a compliant and competitive proposal.

Official Call Link

All relevant documents and the online application form are available at: https://tascrestoremed.eu/community-led-actions/.

']
metadata.title: ['Community-led actions to restore our ocean, seas and waters']

summary: SYNAPSE OPEN CALL
content: SYNAPSE OPEN CALL
citation: Improved monitoring of threats, intrusion detection and response in complex and heterogeneous digital systems and infrastructures. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44114300
metadata.description:
['

The main objective of the SYNAPSE Open Call is to showcase the clear integration paths of SYNAPSE across the EU Digital Infrastructures, validating the relevance & transferability of the platform in additional organisations. Through the SYNAPSE Open Call, interested candidates are invited to present their contributions on how the SYNAPSE Platform could be enriched with additional cybersecurity services or evaluated in industries. In that context, we look for the following two types of entities:

  1. One (1) organisation operating a digital infrastructure, which will serve as end-user and provide a new pilot for the SYNAPSE project (Open Call Beneficiary Type A), preferably in the fields of Electrical Energy, Water, Transportation, Telecommunications, FinTech, Manufacturing, Agrifood, Logistics, etc.
  2. Two (2) SME partners covering the cybersecurity supply-side, which will provide innovative technological solutions and services to the SYNAPSE project (Open Call Beneficiary Type B).

More specifically, the scope of SYNAPSE Open Call is the enhancement of SYNAPSE Intelligent Services, through:

  1. The incorporation of AI Agents in the Situational Awareness lifecycle, such as risk and threat management assisting human operators take prompt and accurate decisions.
  2. The improvement of risk assessment processes through the incorporation of AI and GenAI.  
  3. The development of protection measures against adversarial attacks. Methods and solutions for the protection of AI enablers, such as threat and vulnerability assessments of AI systems, exploiting information from publicly available sources, such as MITRE Atlas1, AI Risk Management Framework2, and ENISA AI cybersecurity challenges3.   
  4. The enhancement of AI and GenAI systems, such as LLM, ML and DL models, robustness against data poisoning attacks and model inference attacks.   
  5. Incorporation of innovative penetration testing methodologies.  

More information available on SYNAPSE website (https://www.synapse-project.eu/open-call/) including submission link. For enquiries please use this email address: info@synapse-project.eu (as subject SYNAPSE OPEN CALL). 

 


']
metadata.title: ['Improved monitoring of threats, intrusion detection and response in complex and heterogeneous digital systems and infrastructures']

summary: [Deadline extended] - Funding for SMEs and start-ups investing in innovation in the biofertilizers and biostimulants sector - CALL FOR APPLICATIONS
content: [Deadline extended] - Funding for SMEs and start-ups investing in innovation in the biofertilizers and biostimulants sector - CALL FOR APPLICATIONS
citation: Interregional Innovation Investments Strand 1. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46039256
metadata.description:
['

This call will be divided into two application strands:

  1. Strand 1 - Tools and services to support the deployment of biofertilizers to support investment cases (IC) of the project.
  2. Strand 2 - Open Innovation and scale up of new innovative biofertilizers.

Strand 1: Tools and services to support the deployment of biofertilizers to support ICs

IC #1: Soil and nutrient management using organic fertilizers from chicken manure. Boosts soil health and crop yields sustainably with chicken manure-based fertilizers. Geographical Areas: Região Centro and Região do Alentejo (Portugal). Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

IC#1: Analysis of market demand for organic fertilizers from chicken manure and creation of a brand. Building a sustainable fertilizer brand through market insight and eco-friendly innovation. Geographical Areas: Região Centro and Região do Alentejo (Portugal). Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

IC #3: Mapping by-products and waste. Development of a geo-localized map of organic waste and by-products to support biofertilizer production and cost analysis. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €50,000

IC#3: Struvite testing with Compost + Biochar. Formulation of a biofertilizer by mixing struvite (a phosphorus-rich by-product of wastewater treatment) with compost and biochar. Geographical Areas: Emilia-Romagna (Italy). Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

IC #3: Biostimulant Production from Compost. Production of liquid biostimulants using compost and biochar, enriched with elements such as amino acids, aimed at creating a nutrient-rich product for sustainable agriculture. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

IC #3: Compost + Biochar Mix Development. Supports suppliers in the formulation and initial testing of biochar-based fertilizers. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

IC #4: Measuring the effect of biofertilizers on Climate Change. Greenhouse trials using organic-origin components to evaluate their effects under conditions like drought, salinity, or extreme temperature. Geographical Area: Catalunya (Spain). Maximum Grant Amount: €40,000

IC #4: Field trials of biofertilizers adapted to specific crops. Trials focusing on fruit trees, vineyards, and olive trees to test the effectiveness and adaptability of tailored biostimulant formulations. Geographical Area: Catalunya (Spain). Maximum Grant Amount: €55,000

IC #6: Imaging the impact of a biostimulant. Imaging various crops in the field post-application of a biostimulant to observe visible effects. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €15,000

IC #6: Temporal monitoring by spectral imaging of foliar development after application of a biostimulant. Monitors the quantity and quality of foliar development in early stages, comparing treated versus untreated plants. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €25,000

IC #6: Cross-Environmental Impact Studies. Evaluates biostimulant-driven changes in key sustainability metrics like soil, water, biodiversity, and fertility. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €20,000

IC #6: Lifecycle Assessment of Biostimulant Applications. Analysis of carbon sequestration dynamics and the monetization potential of biostimulant applications in agriculture. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €15,000

IC #6: Fertilizer Impregnation Analysis. Assesses the prolonged release and assimilation effects of coated (urea-type) biofertilizers in field conditions. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €10,000

IC #6: Agronomic tests on vines (thermal/hydric stress). Evaluates the effects of biostimulants on grapevines to improve resistance to heat and water stress. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €5,500

IC #6: Agronomic tests on vines (nutrient recovery). Assesses how biostimulants affect post-harvest recovery of carbohydrate and mineral nutrient reserves in grapevines. Geographical Areas: Any of the eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €5,500

IC #7: Evaluation of a Bacterial Biostimulant on Yield and Soil Fertility in Exotic Crops. Sustainable crop growth through microbial biostimulants and smarter nitrogen use. Geographical Areas: French Guiana, Guadeloupe, Martinique, Mayotte, Reunion Island, and Saint-Martin (France). Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000

Strand 2- Open Innovation: Development of new products and scale up of new innovative biofertilizers

This strand aims to scale up innovative biofertilizers initiatives (TRL 6 – 8). It supports projects within these innovative product and solutions areas, categorized into the four scopes outlined below:

1) Scale-Up of Production: Development of strategies to increase production capacity while ensuring product quality and consistency is maintained. Regions for Implementation: All eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000 (up to €120,000 for collaborative projects)

2) Innovative Formulants and Formulation Processes: Investigation of new formulation methods aimed at improving the efficacy and application of biofertilizers. This includes techniques such as seed treatment and the development of different physical forms (e.g., granular, pellets, crystals, powder). Regions for Implementation: All eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000 (up to €120,000 for collaborative projects)

3) Evaluation of Products: Assessment of product effectiveness under greenhouse or field conditions, including the characterization of their mode of action and analysis of variability sources. Regions for Implementation: All eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000 (up to €120,000 for collaborative projects)

4) Service-Related Projects: Support product deployment through the development of complementary services. These may include the creation of specific spraying tools, decision-making tools, training programs, or communication strategies. Regions for Implementation: All eligible regions. Maximum Grant Amount: €60,000 (up to €120,000 for collaborative projects)

']
metadata.title: ['Interregional Innovation Investments Strand 1']

citation: European Network of AI Excellence Centres: Expanding the European AI lighthouse (RIA). Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/44129190
metadata.description:
['

The 3rd Open Call aims to expand the role of the dAIEDGE Network of Excellence in the edge AI continuum, encompassing micro-, deep-, and meta-edge, with a focus on standardisation, open-source contributions, and infrastructure development. The Collaborative Projects Support Programme will finance up to 10 Proposals to solve the Industrial Challenges defined by the dAIEDGE Consortium, with the possibility of using resources from the dAIEDGE Virtual Lab or adding new resources to it or to re-use developments of the dAIEDGE project or dAIEDGE use-cases.

Each beneficiary project of dAIEDGE Collaborative Projects Open Call can receive a maximum amount of up to 60.000 € for participating in the up to 7 months of Support Programme, which includes the following stages:

● Stage 1: Plan Phase, during which the beneficiary should deliver an Individual Mentoring Plan (including challenges, research activities and budget). The duration of this stage is up to 1 month and, after completing it, the beneficiary will receive a maximum amount of up to 9.000 €.

● Stage 2: Implementation Phase, which should lead to a Proof of Demonstration. The duration of this stage is up to 6 months and, after completing it, the beneficiary will receive a maximum amount of up to 51.000 €.

']

summary: METU Internships Offer
content: METU Internships Offer
citation: Specialised education programmes in key capacity areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46214053
metadata.description:
['

Within the framework of the SUSA project, two partners—METU and GNOMON Informatics SA —will offer internships to degree program students. Below is a consolidated explanation of the criteria, procedures, and financial mechanisms that will govern the provision of third-party financial support related to these internships.

For METU, the maximum amount of financial support for each third-party recipient is set at €610 per internship, in accordance with internal standards for internship remuneration and as aligned with the granting authority’s guidelines.

This call (as well as the financial support that will be provided through it) is NOT an EU action. This call is launched solely by MIDDLE EAST TECHNICAL UNIVERSITY (METU). The financial support to the selected successful applicants will be provided directly by METU in its own name and under its own responsibility. METU’s own responsibility includes the preparation and drafting of this call as well as the selection of successful applicants that will receive the said financial support. The publication of this call in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal is made solely for information purposes with the aim to increase the visibility of this call to the general public, including to potential applicants (i.e. students). The said publication shall not be construed as direct or indirect approval of the content and conditions of this call by the relevant EU authorities or as any form of waiver from the above-described responsibility of METU. 

METU’s financial support amount is derived from internal remuneration benchmarks for student internships. 

For both METU and GNOMON, internships must be aligned with the thematic focus of the SUSA project, including but not limited to: 

  1. Digital health and healthcare innovation 
  2. Health data management and interoperability 
  3. Artificial intelligence applications in healthcare 
  4. Advanced digital skills development and practical application of SUSA educational content 

Internship positions will be open to students enrolled in degree programs, with a focus on engineering and health-related disciplines that are relevant to the SUSA learning objectives. 

To align with academic cycles and optimize visibility, seven internship calls are planned throughout the project. Announcement will be published in June 2025 before the start of the Fall 2025-2026 academic semester with the following cutoff dates: 

  1. September 2025 
  2. January 2026 
  3. September 2026 
  4. January 2027 
  5. September 2027 
  6. January 2028 
  7. September 2028  
']
metadata.title: ['Specialised education programmes in key capacity areas']

citation: Specialised education programmes in key capacity areas. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/competitive-calls-cs/46214053
metadata.description:
['

Within the framework of the SUSA project, two partners—METU and GNOMON Informatics SA —will offer internships to degree program students. Below is a consolidated explanation of the criteria, procedures, and financial mechanisms that will govern the provision of third-party financial support related to these internships.

For GNOMON, the maximum amount of financial support is set at €1,500 per month of internship, calculated based on an estimated structure of 20 internships with a 4-month duration each.

  1. In cases where internships are implemented with a shorter duration than four months, the remaining budget may be reallocated to fund additional internship placements, until the total allocated amount has been fully utilized.

Basis for Financial Calculation

  1. GNOMON’s amount is based on a combination of the company’s standard cost model for internships and the actual licensing cost of the software platform used in SUSA’s shared learning experiences.

For both METU and GNOMON, internships must be aligned with the thematic focus of the SUSA project, including but not limited to: 

  1. Digital health and healthcare innovation 
  2. Health data management and interoperability 
  3. Artificial intelligence applications in healthcare 
  4. Advanced digital skills development and practical application of SUSA educational content 

Eligible Participants 

Internship positions will be open to students enrolled in degree programs, with a focus on engineering and health-related disciplines that are relevant to the SUSA learning objectives. 

This call (as well as the financial support that will be provided through it) is NOT an EU action. This call is launched solely by Gnomon Informatics SA (Gnomon) . The financial support to the selected successful applicants will be provided directly by Gnomon in its own name and under its own responsibility. Gnomon’s own responsibility includes the preparation and drafting of this call as well as the selection of successful applicants that will receive the said financial support. The publication of this call in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal is made solely for information purposes with the aim to increase the visibility of this call to the general public, including to potential applicants (i.e. students). The said publication shall not be construed as direct or indirect approval of the content and conditions of this call by the relevant EU authorities or as any form of waiver from the above-described responsibility of Gnomon. 


To align with academic cycles and optimize visibility, seven internship calls are planned throughout the project. Announcement will be published on June 2025 before the start of the Fall 2025 - 2026 academic semester with the following cutoff dates:  

  1. September 2025 
  2. January 2026 
  3. September 2026 
  4. January 2027 
  5. September 2027

summary: Bridges
content: Bridges
citation: EIT Cross-KIC Strategic Outreach. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/portal/screen/opportunities/topic-details/11168COMPETITIVE_CALLen
metadata.description:
['

The Bridges programme is designed to increase EU-Israel collaboration and the number of EU-Israel submissions and winning consortia. This edition will focus on high-impact open calls in Aquaculture and Civil Security. The main objectives are:

  1. Strengthen collaboration between the EU and Israel and increase the number of EU-Israeli submissions and winning consortia under the Horizon Europe funding programme.
  2. Deepen understanding of Horizon Europe’s open calls in Aquaculture and Civil Security thematic areas, including strategic priorities, eligibility, and evaluation insights.
  3. Enhance the capacity of experienced participants by strengthening the skills, confidence, and proposal-readiness of coordinators and expert partners.
  4. Sharpen project and consortium management skills by learning practical tools on budgeting, deliverables, timelines, and risk management from experts.
  5. Engaging in structured matchmaking and in-person networking to build strong, cross-border partnerships, both during and following the event.
']

metadata.description:
["

The official Master’s degree in Machine Learning and Cybersecurity for Internet-Connected System aims to provide advanced and specialised scientific and technological training in artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with an emphasis on their application in internet-connected systems, including computer systems of multiple degrees of complexity in general and Internet of Things systems in particular. It addresses the current shortage of professionals who have this kind of training, which is highly valued in industry and research in Information and Communications Technology (ICT). The master's program is mainly practical and focused on both application and theoretical foundation. Contrary to more specialized programs, this master's degree offers a broader training in three interrelated and complementary areas that are essential in today's ICT, which are AI, cybersecurity and Internet of Things.

"]

summary: EIT RawMaterials – ERMA Booster Call
content: EIT RawMaterials – ERMA Booster Call
citation: EIT Raw Materials Business Plan 2023-2025. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/data/topicDetails/9942COMPETITIVE_CALLen
metadata.description:
['


As the world’s largest innovation community in the raw materials sector and a recognised impact investor, EIT RawMaterials provides an unparalleled platform to expedite and expand new businesses. Having supported over 400 start-ups, we are continuously elevating the most innovative raw materials solutions globally.

EIT RawMaterials is an impact investor and a unique global raw materials ecosystem, offering full-spectrum support from the earliest stages to Series A funding. Partnering with EIT RawMaterials presents an exceptional opportunity for start-ups, scale-ups SMEs and larger raw material extraction, processing and recycling project to scale their company towards global success. In addition we pledge tailored support to the most promising ventures, combining funding and expert services to enhance business growth and scalability.

As the demand for cutting-edge raw materials solutions grows, EIT RawMaterials identifies and empowering companies that are revolutionizing the sector with breakthrough technologies and sustainable practices. Through our ERMA Booster Call, we invite visionary founders to join our movement. Through this call, eligible companies with advanced, innovative extraction, processing and recycling projects are also invited to apply for funding and collaborate with EIT RawMaterials to reach key milestones in their project’s development.

We’re propelling early-stage start-ups to market breakthroughs to miners and recyclers, and aiding more advanced enterprises in reaching vital milestones.


The present call is open throughout 2024 and 2025 and EIT RawMaterials is looking for start-ups, scale-ups and SMEs to grow jointly until exit. We\'re offering investments of up to €2.500.000 in funding and provide comprehensive value-added services, all aimed at delivering a lasting, sustainable impact on the raw materials value chain.

']
metadata.title: ['EIT Raw Materials Business Plan 2023-2025']

summary: Call for marine biodiversity (monitoring) data
content: Call for marine biodiversity (monitoring) data
citation: Integration of biodiversity monitoring data into the Digital Twin Ocean. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/data/topicDetails/9642COMPETITIVE_CALLen
metadata.description:
['

The selected beneficiaries will establish ingestion pipelines of marine biodiversity data, and thereby increase the flow of relevant biodiversity data to the EU DTO.


The selected beneficiaries commit to carry out the following activities:

  1. Participate in the workshop which will provide training on how to reformat and quality control data conform international standards (see further) and make these available to EMODnet Biology, held from the 3rd of June to the 5th of June 2025 in Paris (3 full days). In-person attendance of at least one representative per selected beneficiary is required. It will also be possible to attend the training workshop online, in case multiple people per beneficiary want to attend the workshop but cannot all attend in person.
  2. Complete the online training course provided by EMODnet Biology (required for at least one representative per selected beneficiary).
  3. Contribute to the flow of marine biodiversity data into the DTO, through developing data flow pipelines to EMODnet Biology. Setting up a data flow through intermediate data systems that already have or will in the near future have a direct data flow pipeline to EMODnet Biology (e.g. ICES, ETN) is also eligible.
  4. Optimize the data flow so that it is as much as possible automated and commit to continued data submission, ensuring sustained data flows to the DTO.
  5. Ensure the data is formatted, standardized, and quality-controlled conforming to the relevant international standards (as demonstrated in the workshop and the EMODnet Biology online training course) (e.g. using the Darwin Core standard for biodiversity data, OBIS-ENV-DATA as the preferred format, World Register of Marine Species, Marine Regions and BODC NERC Vocabulary Server (NVS) to standardize taxonomic names, location names and parameter names respectively, EMODnet Biology QC Check tool to perform quality checks).
  6. Ensure that metadata and data will become available through EMODnet Biology. Data need to be published on an Integrated Publishing Toolkit (IPT) instance and pass the EMODnet Biology Quality Control process.
  7. Ensure the data complies to Open Access, preferably under a Creative Commons CC-BY license. If applicants plan to use any other license, this needs to be detailed in their application.
  8. Provide short intermediary reports on their progress every four months, and a final report on the results at the end of the 12-month project.


Timeline

March 31st 2025: Evaluation completed & beneficiaries informed

From May 1st 2025 to April 30th 2026 (12 months): Project implementation

June 3rd-5th 2025: Mandatory attendance of the planned workshop

August 31st 2025: First interim report + online course completion deadline

December 31st 2025: Second interim report deadline

April 30th 2026: Final report, data available and project completion deadline

']
metadata.title: ['Integration of biodiversity monitoring data into the Digital Twin Ocean']

metadata.description:
['

The Call for financial support to third parties targets educational institutions. Any interested school or university can apply for support to carry out one or more of the following activities:

  1. Preparation of informational materials for students in general about cybersecurity and their role in ensuring cyber resilience in everyday life,
  2. Software tools for simulating cyber threats,
  3. Organization of workshops for students on how to detect and respond to cybersecurity incidents,
  4. Organization of conferences and inviting cybersecurity experts as speakers.

More information about the Call can be found on the project website: https://www.erstebank.hr/hr/sigurnost/erste-cyber-guardian and Financiranje potpore trećim stranama (erstebank.hr)

']

summary: EIT Food Impact Funding Framework
content: EIT Food Impact Funding Framework
citation: EIT Food Business Plan 2023-2025. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/data/topicDetails/7641COMPETITIVE_CALLen
metadata.description:
['

This Funding Opportunity invites proposals from all sides of the knowledge triangle: innovation, education, and entrepreneurship, but in combination with a key fourth element, citizen/public engagement.

All our funded projects are collaborative and require a minimum of two organisations working together. At least one of these should be a company or social enterprise directly involved with key communities, and at least one other should be a research or university organisation. These organisations should be from at least two different countries eligible to Horizon Europe.


Type of proposals sought in the Single Projects Co-funding Opportunity


We can be flexible in what we fund. While we are always interested in funding new R&D and commercialisation projects with good prospects of getting to market within 12-18 months, we can also provide funding to:

•  Existing research project teams who have a commercialisation or impact plan that needs follow-on funding to get to market. These do not have to be previous EIT Food projects – we can support projects previously funded by other EIT organisations, EU programmes and third-party funders – but we cannot “double fund” other current EU funded projects.

 

•  Develop and market new applications and business models for existing technology. This includes transferring proven technology from other sectors into the food industry.

 

•  Run market demonstration and scale-up activities for high impact products or services that already have promising results.

 

•  Demonstrate and expand impact from existing innovative social enterprise, dietary health or education programmes and interventions leading to behaviour change or better consumer outcomes.

 

Under this scheme, we encourage proposals from consortia based around lead organisations which have the capabilities to move quickly and effectively in creating impact from our co-investment, supported by high-quality research, communications and impact assessment expertise.


Type of proposals sought in the Collaborative Programmes

We are looking to fund and co-resource alliances and collaborative programmes which aim to produce multiple results and high-impact outcomes. By “programmes” we mean connected activities and workstreams across economic, industry and social value chains. Examples of actions we could support to achieve our Mission Targets might include:

  1. co-funding open competitions aimed at accelerating new business, products and processes
  2. jointly funding, co-resourcing and/or co-delivering programmes of activities with other funders, NGOs or charities with shared impact goals
  3. co-ordinating consortia across whole value-chains to scale-up, demonstrate and/or accelerate impact of innovation solutions from the EIT Food project portfolio
  4. jointly supporting innovation and knowledge exchange clusters that deliver new programmes in citizen engagement, business, skills and agri-food innovation in cities/ regions or specialist sectors.
  5. co-funding social enterprise or education programmes that will scale-up interventions, leading to improved outcomes for populations at risk of poor health or food security outcomes supporting the development of networks to test production/manufacturing capabilities for key markets, test farms/services and “living labs” delivering programmes
  6. collaborative programmes resolving sector-relevant strategic issues to enable stronger progress towards meeting Mission Targets.


This may include intersecting areas such as: standards setting and data management; fundraising for systems change; citizen engagement in innovation; consumer behaviour change and improving public understanding of the benefits of innovation in agri-food. The list above is extensive, but not exhaustive.

']
metadata.title: ['EIT Food Business Plan 2023-2025']

metadata.description:
['

As the world’s largest innovation community in the raw materials sector and a recognised impact investor, EIT RawMaterials provides an unparalleled platform to expedite and expand new businesses. Having supported over 400 startups, we are continuously elevating the most innovative raw materials solutions globally.

EIT RawMaterials is an impact investor and a unique global raw materials ecosystem, offering full-spectrum support from the earliest stages to Series A funding. Partnering with EIT RawMaterials presents an exceptional opportunity for start-ups, scale-ups and SMEs to scale their company towards global success. We pledge tailored support to the most promising ventures, combining funding and expert services to enhance business growth and scalability.

Through our Booster Call, we invite visionary founders to join our movement. We’re propelling early-stage startups to market breakthroughs and aiding more advanced enterprises in reaching vital milestones.


EIT RawMaterials is seeking companies developing solutions (products and services) supporting the three EIT RawMaterials Lighthouse programmes:

1.Responsible Sourcing Lighthouse: leads the innovation in mineral exploration, responsible sourcing and processing of raw materials for Europe’s green future.

2.Sustainable Materials Lighthouse: guides innovation in the making, using, and recycling of sustainable and cost-efficient raw materials for future green mobility and energy.

3.Circular Societies Lighthouse: fosters innovation in the radical shift to a circular economy and society enabling economic growth and carbon-neutrality.

']

summary: OFFERR Call
content: OFFERR Call
citation: European facility for nuclear research. Available at: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/data/topicDetails/3876COMPETITIVE_CALLen
metadata.description:
['

After each cut-off date, the received applications will follow an eligibility check according to the European commission rules (see eligibility criteria) and then through the evaluation procedure (see evaluation criteria).

The selected project proposals will receive the financial support from the coordinator of the OFFERR project in compliance with the decision made by the consortium based on evaluation results, including SNETP.

OFFERR is funded under Horizon Europe / Euratom call and by consequence it applies UE rules and regulations. Regarding participating countries to OFFERR’s call, candidates must comply with UE rules and be included in the list of participating countries in Horizon Europe. The list can be find here . Countries that are not listed as member of HE or third countries associated to HE can still participate to OFFERR’s call but won’t be able to receive funds. The project will have to explain in its proposal how the research team/facility will be supported by national funds.

Detailed information on the proposal requirements, evaluation criteria as well as reporting of the achievements are contained in the following documents:

·       Terms of reference

·       Eligibility criteria

·       Evaluation criteria

·       User facility database (including the clusters of facilities and the fast-track catalogue)

·       Guidelines for applicants

·       Application template

·       User report template

·       Infrastructure report template

']
metadata.title: ['European facility for nuclear research']
